explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in C tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in C tier with an overall score of 42 from a base nutrition score of 42. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: filling for the calories
topPros.explanation: Legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

topPros.title: cheap staple with real payoff
topPros.explanation: This matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often.

topPros.title: fibre-forward category identity
topPros.explanation: Legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

topCons.title: dilution makes it less impressive than whole legumes
topCons.explanation: The legume identity is still there, but not in full strength.

topCons.title: can take planning or prep
topCons.explanation: That is a small but real friction cost.

topCons.title: protein quality still has a ceiling
topCons.explanation: Good, but not meat or dairy level.

closing.summary: Unsweetened Soy Milk (Powder Basis) stands out for filling for the calories and cheap staple with real payoff. but it also gets held back by dilution makes it less impressive than whole legumes and can take planning or prep. Best only when convenience outweighs the weaker fibre or protein payoff.
closing.finalReveal: Unsweetened Soy Milk (Powder Basis) is C tier.
